canonical_definition: Based on the available literature, ComVE is characterized as a natural language reasoning dataset. The task within this dataset requires a model to identify which of two sentences violates common sense. It is explicitly used to evaluate two capabilities: commonsense reasoning and explanation generation. One study situates it among other "widely used natural language reasoning datasets with NLEs," indicating its use in experiments alongside similar instruments.
papers: Bit-Flip Error Resilience inLLMs: A Comprehensive Analysis and Defense Framework
Reasoning-to-Defend: Safety-Aware Reasoning Can Defend Large Language Models from Jailbreaking
When Annotators Disagree, Topology Explains: Mapper, a Topological Tool for Exploring Text Embedding Geometry and Ambiguity

definitions.text: A natural language reasoning dataset where the task is to identify which of two sentences violates common sense, used to evaluate commonsense reasoning and explanation generation.
definitions.paper: When Annotators Disagree, Topology Explains: Mapper, a Topological Tool for Exploring Text Embedding Geometry and Ambiguity
